design.summary: M15 = "Shared memory blocks + session blackboard". A Block is a kernel-owned safe-Rust object (new ObjKind::Block) that OWNS a Vec<u64> of M6 frame PAs and is reached as a capability through the M11 HandleTable. Unlike M14 channels (which MOVE/copy a payload), a Block is mapped into MULTIPLE agents' AddressSpaces at once via the already-green M10 map_in_space, so every member sees the SAME physical bytes. Permission is rights-derived at the chokepoint: effective_writable = request_write && handle_rights.contains(WRITE) — WRITE handle -> RW page, READ-only handle -> RO page. The session blackboard is a well-known shared Block all participants attach (|members|=1 is the degenerate single-agent case, no separate code path). Two complementary sharing planes: (1) FRAME plane — the same physical frames mapped into N roots (the literal seL4/Zircon true-sharing demonstration); (2) RECORD plane — a kernel-mediated CAS/versioned record store reached through the shared Rc<Block> (the ROADMAP's "last-write-wins fixed in the kernel", race-free because dispatch runs single-core interrupts-masked). Block bookkeeping is 100% #![forbid(unsafe_code)] safe Rust in a NEW crates/tb-hal/src/blocks.rs (the ipc.rs/mem.rs precedent). The map path reuses M10's existing arch::map_in_root — so the MINIMAL milestone adds ZERO new unsafe; the ONLY candidate new unsafe is an OPTIONAL per-arch unmap_in_root if M_BLOCK_UNMAP/reclamation is implemented now (deferred — pin frames for kernel-session lifetime). Marker: "M15: blocks OK" between main.rs:1741 and 1743.
design.mapping_and_permissions:
THE MAP PATH (100% reuses M10 — ZERO new unsafe for the minimal milestone).

Primitive already green (the only unsafe, untouched): tb_hal::map_in_space(space: AddressSpace, va, pa, writable) -> bool (lib.rs:999) -> arch::map_in_root(space.root, va, pa, writable) (x86_64/mmu.rs:667 leaf=PTE_P|PTE_NX|(PTE_RW if writable), KERNEL-accessible, NO U/S; aarch64/mmu.rs:733 leaf=Normal-WB EL1, +AP[2]=1 RO when !writable).

ATTACH a Block into agent T at base_va (one map_in_space per frame):
  fn agent_block_map(t, h, want_writable, base_va) -> (status, va):
    1. let ap = AGENTS.slot(t.raw()).as_mut()?;            // gives BOTH ap.table AND ap.space (M10 root)
    2. let (rights, blk) = match ap.table.block_of(h) { Ok(x)=>x, Err(e)=>return (e,0) };   // Stale/BadCap surfaced
    3. if !rights.contains(Rights::READ) { return (Denied,0) }                              // the READ gate
    4. let writable = want_writable && rights.contains(Rights::WRITE);                       // permission = MIN(request, rights)
    5. for i in 0..blk.n_pages() { if !map_in_space(ap.space, base_va + (i as u64)*0x1000, blk.frames()[i], writable) { return (NoMem,0) } }
    6. blk.record_member(ap.space.root_pa(), base_va, writable);                             // safe bookkeeping (refcount/detach)
    7. return (Ok, base_va)

Because the leaf lands in a NON-live, per-agent root in a top-level slot the kernel half leaves VACANT, it never touches the kernel half or another agent — the exact isolation M10/M12 already rely on. Mapping the SAME blk.frames()[i] into both agent_c's and agent_d's roots makes both roots translate base_va to the SAME physical frame => true sharing.

PERMISSION DERIVATION = min(request, handle rights), enforced at the chokepoint via the existing Rights::contains predicate:
- M_BLOCK_MAP gated on READ (must read to map). The page WRITE bit is granted ONLY if the handle ALSO holds WRITE: writable = want_writable && rights.contains(WRITE). A READ-only Block handle requesting write -> RO mapping (write bit silently dropped — the literal "min"). This is identical to Zircon vmar_map's layered-rights min and seL4's diminished-frame-cap discipline.
- M_BLOCK_WRITE gated on WRITE: a READ-only handle is rejected Denied at the required_right gate (no body runs) — the exact M14 "WRITE-less endpoint -> Denied" precedent (main.rs:1673).
- Attenuation flows downhill only: M_HANDLE_NARROW(mask=READ) on a Block handle hands a peer a RO view; the monotonic-AND invariant (Rights::intersect, narrow at caps.rs:548) means a RO sharee can never re-amplify to RW.

VA WINDOW: base_va must sit in a VACANT top-level slot clear of the agent's own code/stack (AGENT_CODE_VA=0x0000_0200_0000_0000 PML4[4], code@+0x0 stack@+0x1000 on x86_64; L1[6]=0x0000_0001_8000_0000 on aarch64). Recommend a DEDICATED vacant sibling slot with a const-assert layout lock (the USER_*/CAPS_* const-assert precedent in user.rs:682): x86_64 BLOCK_WINDOW_VA = 0x0000_0280_0000_0000 (PML4[5]); aarch64 0x0000_0001_C000_0000 (L1[7]); `const _: () = assert!((BLOCK_WINDOW_VA>>39)&0x1FF == 5);`. A late-joining member re-maps deterministically from blk.frames() (each agent root is a COPY of the kernel half taken at create time, so each member maps SEPARATELY).

EL0-VISIBILITY NOTE: map_in_space leaves are KERNEL-only (no U/S / EL1), so an EL0 agent cannot itself load/store the block through them — the self-test rides KERNEL-side I/O under each root (the M10/M13/M14 facade pattern). The EL0-visible variant just swaps in arch::map_user_in_root (x86_64/mmu.rs:724, aarch64/mmu.rs:780; ALREADY EXISTS, pub(super), U/S/AP[1]=1) behind a NEW `pub fn map_user_in_space(space, va, pa, writable)` lib facade into the agent's PML4[4]/L1[6] user window at AGENT_CODE_VA+0x10000 — purely ADDITIVE, STILL zero new unsafe (the primitive is already audited M12 code).
design.abi:
Pointer-free [u64;4] SyscallArgs through the single M11 dispatch chokepoint, rights-gated via required_right, continuing the closed numbered space (M14 used 25/26/27 -> M15 uses 28/29/30/31).

NUMBERED METHOD CONSTS (caps.rs, after M_CHAN_CLOSE=27):
  pub const M_BLOCK_MAP:   u32 = 28;  // attach the block into the CALLER's address space
  pub const M_BLOCK_UNMAP: u32 = 29;  // voluntary member detach (DEFERRED — see new_unsafe)
  pub const M_BLOCK_WRITE: u32 = 30;  // RECORD-plane CAS/versioned write
  pub const M_BLOCK_READ:  u32 = 31;  // RECORD-plane read latest

required_right additions (caps.rs:362 table — keeps the method space TOTAL):
  M_BLOCK_MAP   => Rights::READ,
  M_BLOCK_UNMAP => Rights::NONE,     // detaching your own mapping is always allowed
  M_BLOCK_WRITE => Rights::WRITE,
  M_BLOCK_READ  => Rights::READ,

DISPATCH ARMS (caps.rs:687, the Rc-clone-out-then-act discipline used by M_MEM_*/M_CHAN_*):
- M_BLOCK_WRITE: clone Object Rc out + drop slot borrow; match &obj.block { Some(b)=> b.cas_write(args[0]/*off*/, args[1]/*val*/, args[2]/*expected version*/).map(SysReturn::ok).unwrap_or(SysReturn::err(WouldBlock/*CAS lost*/)), None=>BadCap }.
- M_BLOCK_READ: same clone-out; match &obj.block { Some(b)=> b.read_latest(args[0]).map(SysReturn::ok).unwrap_or(err(BadCap)), None=>BadCap }.
- M_BLOCK_MAP/M_BLOCK_UNMAP are ADDRESS-SPACE-dependent (dispatch only holds &mut HandleTable, not the AddressSpace), so their BODY rides a kernel facade — the agent_chan_recv_full precedent (lib.rs, a facade that reaches a table method directly because the single-scalar dispatch path is insufficient). The facade re-enforces required_right(M_BLOCK_MAP)==READ and writable=req&&contains(WRITE), so the chokepoint algebra is REUSED not duplicated. Numbers 28/29 stay registered for the future EL0 syscall gate (which DOES know the current agent and thus its AddressSpace and can route 28 -> the same facade).

KERNEL-MEDIATED FACADES (lib.rs, no numbered method — the agent_channel_connect precedent at lib.rs:1421, two SEPARATE AGENTS.slot scopes so no two &mut AgentProcess overlap):
  pub fn agent_block_create(a: Task, b: Task, n_pages: usize) -> Option<(Handle, Handle)>
      // blocks::Block::create(n_pages)?; then in scope{ap_a.table.mint_block(READ|WRITE|TRANSFER|DUP, blk.clone())} and scope{ap_b.table.mint_block(READ|WRITE, blk)} — Rc cloned across both. Role rights: full member RW, observer READ-only.
  pub fn agent_block_map(t: Task, h: Handle, want_writable: bool, base_va: u64) -> Option<(u32,u64)>   // the map path above
  pub fn agent_block_dispatch(t: Task, method: u32, h: Handle, a0: u64, a1: u64, a2: u64) -> Option<(u32,u64)>  // thin wrapper over caps::dispatch for M_BLOCK_WRITE/READ from the self-test (the agent_cap_dispatch precedent, lib.rs:1484)
  pub fn session_blackboard() -> Option<Handle>   // returns the well-known session Block handle to attach

ALL args are inline scalars (n_pages, want_writable flag, base_va, record off/val/expected-version) — pointer-free, consistent with the M11/M14 inline ABI. The closed status surface is reused unchanged: Ok / BadCap (block==None) / BadMethod (>=32) / Denied (gate) / Stale (revoked) / NoMem (frame OOM) / WouldBlock (CAS lost). No new SysStatus.
design.blackboard:
THE SESSION BLACKBOARD = one WELL-KNOWN shared Block every session participant attaches (the classic Hearsay-II/BB1 blackboard: knowledge-source agents communicate ONLY through the shared space, never by addressing each other). Per docs/AGENTS-SPEC.md §6/§8 and docs/ROADMAP-V2.md:244-252: "update-once-visible-everywhere ... the last-write-wins library bug fixed in the kernel ... on conflict keep BOTH versions bi-temporally with a supersedes link ... a single-agent session is just |members|=1, no separate code path".

INSTANTIATION:
1. At session setup the kernel creates ONE Block (blocks::Block::create) and mints an Rc-clone Block handle into EACH member's HandleTable with role-derived rights — contributors get READ|WRITE (RW), observers get READ (RO via narrow) — pure M11 attenuation, no new mechanism.
2. Each member attaches via M_BLOCK_MAP at the agreed BLOCK_WINDOW_VA. Because all members map the SAME blk.frames(), a write by one member is physically visible to all others (FRAME plane).
3. STRUCTURED coordination uses the RECORD plane: M_BLOCK_WRITE(off, val, expected_version) does a kernel-mediated compare-and-swap on records[]; on a losing CAS it APPENDS a superseding Rec (keeps both, supersedes link = M13's bi-temporal append-only pattern reused at record granularity) and bumps seq; M_BLOCK_READ(off) returns the latest. This is the ONLY way to actually enforce update-once-visible-everywhere — an honor-system userspace library over raw bytes cannot. It is trivially atomic w.r.t. preemption because dispatch runs single-core with interrupts masked (the RefCell soundness argument M13/M14 already discharge); real SMP CAS + watch-wakeups (a writer bumps seq then signals waiters over an M14 channel — "watch-wakeups") are the ADDITIVE Step-2 layer, exactly as M14 deferred recv-blocks-off-runqueue.

WHY TWO PLANES: the FRAME plane satisfies "two agents see the SAME bytes" (the literal shared-memory block, demonstrated kernel-side in the self-test); the RECORD plane satisfies the ROADMAP's race-free CAS/versioned/bi-temporal blackboard and works even though kernel-leaf pages are not EL0-readable, because the Rc<Block> is shared at the OBJECT level (the M14 Rc-rendezvous, applied to blocks). The CONTROL component of the blackboard pattern (the scheduler that selects which knowledge-source agent runs) is NOT new kernel code — it is an ordinary supervisor agent that watches the block and dispatches members; the kernel only guarantees the race-free shared mapping + atomic publish (Plan 9 "no new API for coordination"). Producer/consumer across the awake agent and the M17 sleep-time consolidation agent share the same block — the blackboard is the seam between foreground and BULK lanes. EXPLICITLY do NOT use copy-on-write for the blackboard: CoW silently diverges the first writer into a private snapshot and breaks visible-everywhere; reserve a CoW child-block for M17/M18 fork-sandbox snapshots.
design.module_location:
SAFE-RUST BLOCK BOOKKEEPING lives in a NEW module C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\crates\tb-hal\src\blocks.rs with header `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` — the exact ipc.rs/mem.rs precedent. It holds the entire Block core: frames: Vec<u64>, n_pages, members: RefCell<Vec<(u64,u64,bool)>>, seq: Cell<u64>, records: RefCell<Vec<Rec>>, plus create()/cas_write()/read_latest()/record_member()/accessors. ALL of it is ordinary safe heap (Vec/Cell/RefCell/Rc) — zero unsafe, the same soundness argument ipc.rs discharges (payload reached through a shared Rc<Object>, sound because dispatch runs single-core interrupts-masked so there is no reentrant borrow).

FILES TO TOUCH (all absolute):
- NEW  C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\crates\tb-hal\src\blocks.rs — the safe Block core (above).
- EDIT C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\crates\tb-hal\src\caps.rs — ObjKind::Block (append after Generic, ~line 218); Object.block field + block:None in the 4 literals (mint:480, mint_memory_home:490, mint_record:504, mint_channel_endpoint:524); HandleTable::mint_block + HandleTable::block_of (~line 520); consts M_BLOCK_MAP=28/UNMAP=29/WRITE=30/READ=31 (after :361); required_right arms (:362 table); dispatch arms for M_BLOCK_WRITE/READ (:687, clone-Rc-out-then-act). Stays #![forbid(unsafe_code)].
- EDIT C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\crates\tb-hal\src\lib.rs — `mod blocks;` next to mod ipc/mod mem (:42-43); facades agent_block_create / agent_block_map / agent_block_dispatch / session_blackboard; BLOCK_WINDOW_VA const + layout-lock assert; OPTIONAL map_user_in_space facade exposing arch::map_user_in_root for the EL0-visible variant.
- EDIT C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\kernel\src\main.rs — the M15 self-test between line 1741 and 1743; marker "M15: blocks OK". ALSO: the M14 negative test at main.rs:1694 uses method 28 expecting BadMethod — change 28 -> 33 (a still-unclaimed number) since M_BLOCK_MAP now claims 28.
- DOCS  docs/ROADMAP-V2.md:244 (M15 marker "M15: blocks OK"); docs/AGENTS-SPEC.md §6/§8 (blackboard = memory:session/<sess> + shared blocks).

REUSED AS-IS (zero change): map_in_space (lib.rs:999) / arch::map_in_root (x86_64/mmu.rs:667, aarch64/mmu.rs:733); frame_alloc (lib.rs:629); address_space_switch_root (lib.rs:1024) / address_space_switch_default (lib.rs:1012); addr_store_load (lib.rs:1080) / addr_load (lib.rs:1099); agent_root_pa (lib.rs:1570); caps::dispatch chokepoint; M_HANDLE_NARROW / agent_mint_generic (lib.rs:1509) / agent_rights_of (lib.rs:1521) for attenuation tests; AgentProcess.space + .table (lib.rs:1190); AGENTS.slot (lib.rs:1229).
design.new_unsafe:
NONE for the minimal milestone — the ROADMAP's "Zero new unsafe" claim HOLDS for map+share. blocks.rs and caps.rs are #![forbid(unsafe_code)]; the VA->PA mapping reuses the already-green M10 arch::map_in_root (x86_64/mmu.rs:667, aarch64/mmu.rs:733), whose existing unsafe is untouched. The optional EL0-visible variant reuses arch::map_user_in_root (x86_64/mmu.rs:724, aarch64/mmu.rs:780) — ALREADY-EXISTING M12 unsafe, merely re-exported behind a safe map_user_in_space facade, so still zero NEW unsafe.

THE ONE PLACE NEW UNSAFE COULD APPEAR (and why it stays confined to tb-hal arch mmu): correct HARD REVOCATION / detach / frame reclamation needs to retract a PTE already installed in a victim root, and NO unmap primitive exists anywhere in tb-hal today (grep-confirmed: no unmap_in_root / clear_entry). Implementing M_BLOCK_UNMAP or drop-time frame_free therefore requires a NEW per-arch primitive `pub fn unmap_in_root(root_pa, va) -> bool` in crates/tb-hal/src/arch/x86_64/mmu.rs and crates/tb-hal/src/arch/aarch64/mmu.rs that walks the explicit (non-live) root to the leaf, writes a not-present entry (write_volatile of 0), and invalidates the TLB if that root is live (invlpg on x86_64 / `tlbi vae1is` + dsb/isb on aarch64). This MUST be unsafe because it dereferences raw page-table frame pointers and mutates live translation — exactly the same reason map_in_root is unsafe, and it sits in the SAME file beside it. It stays in tb-hal arch mmu because page-table memory and TLB maintenance are the HAL's sole responsibility; the Block bookkeeping (members list, refcount, frame_free decision, the soft-revoke generation bump via the existing M_HANDLE_REVOKE) is all SAFE Rust above it.

RECOMMENDATION for M15: PIN and DEFER. Map+share only; soft-revoke (M_HANDLE_REVOKE bumps the slot generation, stopping FUTURE M_BLOCK_MAP/WRITE/READ on that handle) is sufficient for the milestone and is already implemented. Do NOT frame_free on Block drop while any member still maps the frames (no unmap exists => a freed-then-reused frame behind a stale PTE = cross-agent use-after-free). Blocks stay pinned for the kernel-session lifetime (the M14 copy_to_user deferral precedent). Budget the unmap_in_root primitive only when M_BLOCK_UNMAP / live reclamation is actually required (M17/M18), at which point it is the sole, small, well-contained new unsafe.
risks: map_in_space/map_in_root produces KERNEL-only pages (no U/S on x86_64; EL1 on aarch64) — an EL0 agent CANNOT load/store the block through them. The self-test MUST ride kernel-side I/O under each root (address_space_switch_root + addr_store_load/addr_load), exactly as M10/M13/M14 did. True EL0-visible sharing requires exposing arch::map_user_in_root (already exists, pub(super), U/S/AP[1]=1) via a new map_user_in_space facade into the agent's user window — additive, still zero new unsafe, but a real design decision to flag and likely the right default for real agents.

NO unmap primitive exists anywhere in tb-hal (grep-confirmed). M_BLOCK_UNMAP and frame reclamation are therefore DEFERRED (blocks pinned for kernel-session lifetime) OR require the sole new unsafe of the milestone (per-arch unmap_in_root: clear leaf PTE + TLB invalidate). Minimal M15 must pin and defer; do NOT call frame_free on Block::drop while a stale PTE still maps the frame, or a reused frame behind a stale PTE = cross-agent use-after-free.

x86_64 CR0.WP=0 at boot (boot.rs sets CR0=PE|PG, bit16 clear) => a RING0 (kernel) write to an RO-mapped (RW=0) page does NOT fault. So the hardware RO-write-fault is NOT reliably observable kernel-side on x86_64 (it WOULD fault from EL0, and at EL1 on aarch64 where AP[2] is enforced). The PORTABLE RO-rejection in the self-test MUST be the CAP-LAYER Denied (M_BLOCK_WRITE on a READ-only handle -> Denied at the gate; M_BLOCK_MAP write-request -> downgraded to RO via min(request,rights)), NOT a kernel page fault.

M_BLOCK_MAP needs the caller's AddressSpace, which caps::dispatch (signature: &mut HandleTable) does NOT have. Resolve by riding a kernel facade agent_block_map (the agent_chan_recv_full precedent) that reaches both ap.table AND ap.space and re-enforces required_right(M_BLOCK_MAP)==READ + writable=req&&contains(WRITE) — do NOT silently duplicate the rights logic; reuse the required_right table so the gate stays single-sourced. Method 28 stays registered for the future EL0 syscall gate that will thread the AddressSpace through.

The M14 negative test at main.rs:1694 dispatches method 28 expecting BadMethod; M_BLOCK_MAP now CLAIMS 28, so that assertion will flip to a real result. Change that test's literal from 28 to 33 (a still-unclaimed number) or the M14 self-test regresses.

Adding Object.block touches all 4 existing Object{} literals (mint:480, mint_memory_home:490, mint_record:504, mint_channel_endpoint:524) — each needs block:None or it will not compile. Dispatch arms for M_BLOCK_WRITE/READ MUST clone the Rc<Object> out and drop the slot/RefCell borrow BEFORE touching block.records (the M_MEM_*/M_CHAN_* single-&mut-at-a-time discipline) to avoid a reentrant borrow_mut.

ObjKind::Block must be APPENDED after Generic (the current last variant) to keep #[repr(u32)] discriminants stable, because M_OBJECT_INSPECT returns kind as a raw u64. Do NOT reuse ObjKind::Skill — it is tagged '(M15)' but is the T4 procedural-memory kind, a documented mislabel; overloading it would conflate procedural skills with shared-memory blocks.

BLOCK_WINDOW_VA must sit in a VACANT top-level slot clear of AGENT_CODE_VA (code@+0x0, stack@+0x1000 in PML4[4]/L1[6]); a collision corrupts the agent's own code/stack mapping. Use a fresh vacant sibling slot (x86_64 PML4[5]=0x0000_0280_0000_0000; aarch64 L1[7]=0x0000_0001_C000_0000) with a const-assert layout lock. Each agent root is a COPY of the kernel half taken at create time, so the single shared Block must be mapped into EACH member's root SEPARATELY (N map_in_space calls), and any member spawned AFTER block creation must re-map deterministically from blk.frames() — keep the frame list in the Block core.

CoW must NOT be used for the blackboard: copy-on-write silently diverges the first writer into a private snapshot and breaks update-once-visible-everywhere. Use TRUE sharing (same frames into N roots) for M15; reserve a distinct CoW child-block variant for M17 consolidation working-copies / M18 fork-sandboxes.

recon.block_object:
A Block = one or more M6 physical frames (`tb_hal::frame_alloc() -> Option<u64>` PA, 4 KiB each; facade at lib.rs:629) PINNED for the session, mapped into MULTIPLE agent roots at once so every member sees the SAME bytes (vs M14 channels which COPY/MOVE). The bookkeeping is 100% SAFE Rust.

SHAPE (mirror the M13 mem.rs / M14 ipc.rs "safe body behind an ObjKind" precedent exactly):
- NEW safe module `C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\crates\tb-hal\src\blocks.rs` with `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` (the ipc.rs/mem.rs precedent). Holds the kernel-owned core:
    `pub struct Block { frames: Vec<u64>, n_pages: usize, version: Cell<u64> /* for CAS/versioned writes */, /* optional: records: RefCell<Vec<(u64,u64,u64)>> for the blackboard CAS layer */ }`
    `pub fn create(n_pages: usize) -> Option<Rc<Block>>` — frame_alloc()s n_pages frames (fail-closed -> None on OOM, freeing any partial alloc), stores their PAs in `frames`. Held behind `Rc<Block>` so every member's table holds an Rc-clone of the SAME core — the EXACT `Rc<ipc::Channel>` rendezvous pattern (ipc.rs:58 `create`).
- `crates\tb-hal\src\caps.rs`: add `ObjKind::Block` (append after `Generic` to keep prior discriminants stable; note: the existing `ObjKind::Skill // (M15)` at caps.rs is a "procedural skill" mislabel, NOT the shared-memory object — add a distinct `Block`). Add a payload field to `Object` (caps.rs ~line 230): `block: Option<Rc<crate::blocks::Block>>`, EXACTLY paralleling the existing `mem: Option<RefCell<MemSubstrate>>` and `chan: Option<(Rc<ipc::Channel>,u8)>`. Every existing `Object { .. }` literal (mint, mint_memory_home, mint_record, mint_channel_endpoint) must gain `block: None`.
- Add `HandleTable::mint_block(rights, chan: Rc<Block>) -> Option<Handle>` — the `mint_channel_endpoint` twin (caps.rs ~line 560): `self.alloc(rights, Rc::new(Object{ kind: ObjKind::Block, mem:None, scalar:0, chan:None, block:Some(block) }))`.

REACHED AS A CAPABILITY: an agent holds a `Block` Handle in its per-principal HandleTable (M11). It is reached ONLY through the M11 dispatch chokepoint (caps.rs `dispatch`). Sharing = give each member table an Rc-clone Block handle (kernel facade `agent_block_create`, the `agent_channel_connect` precedent at lib.rs:1421). The frames are owned by the Rc<Block>; the object lives while any handle references it (Zircon refcount, same as Channel/MemoryHome).
recon.mapping_path:
The actual VA->PA mapping ALREADY EXISTS as M10 and is 100% reused — ZERO new unsafe for the map path:

PRIMITIVE (tb-hal, the only unsafe, already green):
- x86_64: `arch::map_in_root(root_pa, va, pa, writable)` at crates\tb-hal\src\arch\x86_64\mmu.rs:667 (leaf = PTE_P | PTE_NX | (PTE_RW if writable); kernel-accessible, no U/S).
- aarch64: same signature at crates\tb-hal\src\arch\aarch64\mmu.rs:733 (leaf = Normal-WB, EL1 RW, +AP[2] read-only when !writable, no-execute).
- SAFE FACADE already exported: `tb_hal::map_in_space(space: AddressSpace, va, pa, writable) -> bool` at lib.rs:999 -> `arch::map_in_root(space.root, va, pa, writable)`.

EXACT PATH to attach a Block into agent T's address space (one call per frame):
1. Resolve T's AddressSpace: `AGENTS.slot(T.raw()).space` (root via `space.root_pa()`; public probe `agent_root_pa(task)` at lib.rs:1570).
2. Choose block_va in the VACANT top-level window M10/M12 use for private leaves — x86_64 PML4[4] base AGENT_CODE_VA = 0x0000_0200_0000_0000 (user.rs:733; code @ +0x0, stack @ +0x1000), so blocks at e.g. AGENT_CODE_VA + 0x10000+, or a fresh vacant slot PML4[5]; aarch64 L1[6] base 0x0000_0001_8000_0000 (aarch64/user.rs:677). Add a layout-lock `const _: () = assert!(...)` (the USER_*/CAPS_* const-assert precedent).
3. For i in 0..n_pages: `map_in_space(space, block_va + i*0x1000, block.frames[i], writable)`. Because the leaf lands in a NOT-live, per-agent root in a slot the kernel half leaves vacant, it never touches the kernel half or another agent — the SAME isolation guarantee M10/M12 rely on (mmu.rs comment "private entry there never touches the kernel half").

PERMISSION = min(request, handle rights): `writable = request_write && table.rights_of(block_handle).contains(Rights::WRITE)`. WRITE right -> RW mapping; READ-only handle -> RO mapping (write bit dropped) — the M11 attenuation algebra (Rights::intersect / contains).

CAVEAT (drives the self-test design): map_in_root pages are KERNEL-accessible (no U/S), so an EL0 agent cannot itself load/store the block through them — the marker rides KERNEL-side I/O under each root (address_space_switch_root + addr_store_load/addr_load), exactly as M13/M14 rode kernel-side facades. The EL0-visible variant just swaps in `arch::map_user_in_root` (x86_64/mmu.rs:724, aarch64/mmu.rs:780; already exists, U/S=1) behind a new `map_user_in_space` facade — additive, STILL zero new unsafe.
recon.abi_methods:
Pointer-free `[u64;4]` SyscallArgs through the single M11 `dispatch` chokepoint (caps.rs), rights-gated via `required_right()`, continuing the closed numbered method space (M14 used 25/26/27):

NUMBERED USER METHODS on an EXISTING Block handle (resolved against caller's table, then required_right gate, then body):
- `M_BLOCK_MAP = 28`  -> required_right = Rights::READ. args[0] = requested perm (bit0 read / bit1 write) or writable bool; args[1] = optional requested VA (0 = kernel-chosen). Body: clone Rc<Object> out + drop slot borrow (the M_MEM_*/M_CHAN_* discipline at caps.rs dispatch), read `block` payload (None -> BadCap), compute `writable = req_write && slot.rights.contains(Rights::WRITE)`, loop map_in_space over block.frames into the CALLER's space. Returns block_va in SysReturn.value.
- `M_BLOCK_UNMAP = 29` -> required_right = Rights::NONE (unmapping your own mapping is always allowed). DEFERRED in minimal M15 (no unmap primitive exists — see risks); if implemented it is the SOLE new unsafe.
- `M_BLOCK_WRITE = 30` -> required_right = Rights::WRITE. args[0]=offset/record id, args[1]=value, args[2]=expected-version (CAS). Record-level versioned write into block.records (kernel CAS, single-core atomic now).
- `M_BLOCK_READ  = 31` -> required_right = Rights::READ. args[0]=offset/record id -> SysReturn.value.
(unknown >=32 -> BadMethod, the closed set held; M14's "method 28 -> BadMethod" test becomes "method 32".)

KERNEL-MEDIATED (no target handle to gate on -> facades, the agent_channel_connect precedent, NOT numbered user methods):
- CREATE: `agent_block_create(a, b, n_pages) -> Option<(Handle, Handle)>` — blocks::create(n_pages) then mint_block into EACH agent's table (Rc cloned across two SEPARATE AGENTS.slot scopes so no two &mut AgentProcess overlap), rights per role (full member READ|WRITE|TRANSFER, observer READ).
- BLACKBOARD-ATTACH: `session_blackboard()` returns the well-known session Block's handle; attach = M_BLOCK_MAP it.

required_right() additions (caps.rs): M_BLOCK_MAP=>READ, M_BLOCK_UNMAP=>NONE, M_BLOCK_WRITE=>WRITE, M_BLOCK_READ=>READ. All args are inline scalars (n_pages, perm flags, offset, value, version) — pointer-free, consistent with the M11/M14 inline ABI (the variable-length user byte view rides the deferred copy_to_user, like M14).
recon.blackboard:
Per AGENTS-SPEC.md §6 + §8 (docs\AGENTS-SPEC.md): "Blackboard: memory:session/<sess> + shared blocks: — Letta's 'update once, visible everywhere', race-free via kernel CAS/watch. Producer/consumer: the awake agent and the sleep-time-agent share the same blocks." §8: the Session object = member agent handles + memory:session tier + shared blocks + task fan-out streams + common budget; "a single-agent session = the special case of |members|=1; there is no separate code path." ROADMAP §3 M15 (docs\ROADMAP-V2.md:244): "a session blackboard with update-once-visible-everywhere semantics (the last-write-wins library bug fixed in the kernel)... on conflict keep BOTH versions bi-temporally with a supersedes link."

IMPLEMENTATION: the blackboard is just a WELL-KNOWN shared Block all session participants attach (the classic blackboard architecture). At session creation a single Block is created once (kernel facade `session_blackboard_create(members)`); each member's table receives an Rc-clone Block handle with role-derived rights (full member READ|WRITE, observer READ-only — pure M11 attenuation). Each member MAPs it (M_BLOCK_MAP) into its own root at a well-known block VA. Because all members map the SAME physical frames, an M_BLOCK_WRITE (CAS/versioned) by one member is instantly visible to all others' reads = update-once-visible-everywhere. The race-free CAS is trivially atomic now (dispatch is single-core, interrupts masked — the RefCell discipline M13/M14 rely on); real SMP CAS + watch-wakeups are the additive Step-2 layer (like M14's recv-blocks-off-runqueue). The "keep both versions / supersedes link" is the M13 bi-temporal append-only pattern (mem.rs t_invalid/supersedes) reused at block-record granularity.
recon.permissions_rights:
NO NEW RIGHT NEEDED — reuse the existing structural `Rights::READ` (caps.rs:1<<0) and `Rights::WRITE` (1<<1); `Rights::TRANSFER`/`DUP` already cover sharing/attenuation. ObjKind::Block + READ/WRITE fully express block authority.

RIGHTS-GATING through the M11 dispatch chokepoint (caps.rs `dispatch` + `required_right`):
- M_BLOCK_MAP gated on READ (must at least read to map); the WRITE bit of the resulting mapping is granted ONLY if the handle ALSO holds WRITE -> permission = min(request, handle.rights) computed as `req_write && slot.rights.contains(Rights::WRITE)` (the `Rights::contains` predicate, caps.rs). A READ-only Block handle requesting a writable map -> RO mapping (write attenuated away).
- M_BLOCK_WRITE gated on WRITE: a READ-only handle -> Denied at the required_right gate (the EXACT M14 "WRITE-less endpoint -> Denied" precedent, main.rs:1659).
- M_BLOCK_READ gated on READ.

PERMISSION = min(request, rights) is the same monotonic attenuation M11 proves: rights only ever DROP via Rights::intersect (caps.rs). Cross-agent: a Block handle duped/narrowed (M_HANDLE_NARROW, drop WRITE) before sharing gives an observer RO access — identical to how M14 produced its RO carried cap (main.rs:1593 narrow mask READ|TRANSFER drops WRITE). WRITE right -> RW mapping; READ-only right -> RO mapping, as specified.

CLOSED denial surface (no new SysStatus needed; reuse Ok/BadCap/BadMethod/Denied/Stale): non-Block object presented to M_BLOCK_MAP/WRITE/READ -> body sees block==None -> BadCap (the M14 "non-channel -> BadCap" precedent, main.rs:1665); Block handle narrowed to drop READ -> Denied at the gate; unknown method -> BadMethod; revoked/transferred handle -> Stale.
recon.selftest_plan:
SLOT: kernel\src\main.rs immediately AFTER the M14 marker (line 1741 `serial_write_str("M14: ipc OK\n")`) and BEFORE the final `tb_hal::halt()` (line 1743). REUSE agent_c (spawned main.rs:1378) and agent_d (1379) as session members A and B — no new task slots — exactly as M13/M14 reused them. Timer is already disarmed (single-core, interrupts masked — the discipline the Block bookkeeping needs). Mirror the m14_fail closure + step structure (main.rs:1561+).

STEPS:
1. CREATE: `agent_block_create(agent_c, agent_d, 1)` -> (hb_c with READ|WRITE|TRANSFER in C, hb_d with READ|WRITE in D), both Rc-cloning ONE blocks::create(1) frame. None -> m15_fail.
2. MAP into BOTH roots: M_BLOCK_MAP (request write) on hb_c -> RW at block_va in C's root; same on hb_d -> RW at block_va in D's root. Both resolve to the SAME frame PA. (Kernel-side via an `agent_block_dispatch` facade = the agent_cap_dispatch precedent, OR directly map_in_space(space_c, block_va, frame, true) + map_in_space(space_d, block_va, frame, true).)
3. TRUE SHARING (the north-star, distinguishes from M14's COPY): `address_space_switch_root(agent_root_pa(agent_c))`; `addr_store_load(block_va, 0xB10C)` writes the shared frame under A's mapping; `address_space_switch_root(agent_root_pa(agent_d))`; assert `addr_load(block_va) == 0xB10C` — B reads the SAME bytes A wrote; `address_space_switch_default()`. Print "blocks: A wrote, B read the SAME bytes (true sharing)".
4. RO REJECTION (cap layer, portable — see risks re CR0.WP): NARROW hb_d to drop WRITE (M_HANDLE_NARROW mask READ) -> hb_d_ro; `agent_block_dispatch(agent_d, M_BLOCK_WRITE, hb_d_ro, ...)` must be Denied; M_BLOCK_MAP(request write) on hb_d_ro returns an RO mapping (permission=min(req,rights)). Print "blocks: RO handle write Denied + map downgraded to RO".
5. DENIED/NON-BLOCK paths: M_BLOCK_MAP on a Generic cap (agent_mint_generic) -> BadCap; Block handle narrowed to drop READ -> M_BLOCK_MAP Denied; unknown method 32 -> BadMethod. (M14 denied-path precedent, main.rs:1663-1700.)
6. BLACKBOARD: well-known session Block, both members Rc-clone handles + map; agent_c M_BLOCK_WRITE(off 0, val X); agent_d M_BLOCK_READ(off 0) -> X (update-once-visible-everywhere). single-agent path = same code with members={c}.
7. `tb_hal::serial_write_str("M15: blocks OK\n")` — the DoD marker. All M2-M14 markers continue to print (no regression).
recon.integration_points:

ALL Block bookkeeping is safe Rust.
- `C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\crates\tb-hal\src\caps.rs` — add `ObjKind::Block`; add `block: Option<Rc<crate::blocks::Block>>` to `Object` + `block:None` in the 4 existing literals; add `HandleTable::mint_block` (the mint_channel_endpoint twin, ~line 560); add consts M_BLOCK_MAP=28/UNMAP=29/WRITE=30/READ=31; add their `required_right` arms (~line 400); add dispatch arms (~line 700+, clone-Rc-out-then-act discipline). Stays `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.
- `C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\crates\tb-hal\src\lib.rs` — add `mod blocks;` (next to mod ipc/mod mem); kernel-side facades `agent_block_create` (the agent_channel_connect precedent, lib.rs:1421, two separate AGENTS.slot scopes Rc-cloned across), `agent_block_map`, `agent_block_dispatch` (the agent_cap_dispatch precedent, lib.rs:1484); the block_va const + layout-lock assert; OPTIONAL `map_user_in_space` facade exposing arch::map_user_in_root for the EL0-visible variant.
- `C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\kernel\src\main.rs` — the M15 self-test between line 1741 and 1743; marker "M15: blocks OK".
REUSED AS-IS (zero change): `tb_hal::map_in_space` (lib.rs:999) / `arch::map_in_root` (x86_64/mmu.rs:667, aarch64/mmu.rs:733); `frame_alloc` (lib.rs:629); `address_space_switch_root` (lib.rs:1024), `addr_store_load` (1080), `addr_load` (1099), `agent_root_pa` (1570); `caps::dispatch` chokepoint; M_HANDLE_NARROW / agent_mint_generic / agent_rights_of for attenuation tests.
DOCS: ROADMAP-V2.md §3 M15 (line 244) marker "M15: blocks OK"; AGENTS-SPEC.md §6/§8 (blackboard = memory:session/<sess> + shared blocks).
NEW UNSAFE: NONE for the minimal milestone — blocks.rs + caps.rs are #![forbid(unsafe_code)]; the VA->PA mapping is the already-green M10 arch::map_in_root. The ONLY place M15 could add unsafe is an OPTIONAL per-arch `unmap_in_root(root_pa, va)` (clear leaf PTE + TLB invalidate) in arch\{x86_64,aarch64}\mmu.rs if M_BLOCK_UNMAP/reclamation is implemented now rather than deferred.
risks: map_in_space/map_in_root produces KERNEL-only pages (no U/S bit) — an EL0 agent CANNOT load/store the block through them. The marker MUST ride kernel-side I/O under each root (address_space_switch_root + addr_store_load/addr_load), exactly as M13/M14 rode kernel facades. EL0-visible sharing needs exposing arch::map_user_in_root (x86_64/mmu.rs:724, aarch64/mmu.rs:780; currently pub(super), already exists) via a map_user_in_space facade — additive, still ZERO new unsafe, but a real design decision to flag.

NO unmap primitive exists anywhere in tb-hal (grep confirmed: no unmap_in_root/clear_entry). M_BLOCK_UNMAP and frame reclamation are therefore either DEFERRED (blocks pinned for the kernel session lifetime — the M14 copy_to_user deferral precedent) or require the SOLE new unsafe in the whole milestone: a per-arch unmap_in_root clearing the leaf PTE + TLB-invalidate (invlpg / tlbi). Minimal M15 should pin and defer.

x86_64 CR0.WP=0 (boot.rs sets cr0=PE|PG only; bit 16 clear) => a KERNEL (ring0) write to an RO-mapped (RW=0) page does NOT fault. So the hardware RO-write-fault is NOT reliably observable kernel-side on x86_64 (it WOULD fault from EL0, and at EL1 on aarch64 where AP[2] is enforced). The PORTABLE RO rejection must be the CAP-LAYER Denied (M_BLOCK_WRITE on a READ-only handle -> Denied; M_BLOCK_MAP write-request -> downgraded RO via min(request,rights)), NOT a kernel page fault.

Block VA must sit in the vacant top-level slot (PML4[4]/L1[6]) at an offset clear of AGENT_CODE_VA (code @ +0x0, stack @ +0x1000). A collision corrupts the agent's own code/stack mapping. Use block_va = AGENT_CODE_VA + 0x10000+ or a fresh vacant slot (PML4[5]/L1[7]) with a const-assert layout lock (the USER_*/CAPS_* const-assert precedent in user.rs).

M10 CAVEAT (mmu.rs): each agent root is a COPY of the kernel half taken at create time. A single shared Block must be mapped into EACH member's root SEPARATELY (N map_in_space calls), and any member spawned AFTER the block is created must (re)map it — keep the frames list in the Block core so re-mapping a late-joining member is deterministic.

Refcount/use-after-free: Block frames are owned by Rc<Block>; if the last Block handle drops and blocks::Block::Drop calls frame_free WHILE an agent root still maps the frames (no unmap), the freed frame is reused while a stale PTE still points at it = cross-agent UAF. Until M_BLOCK_UNMAP exists, do NOT frame_free on drop — pin frames for the kernel session lifetime.

ObjKind::Skill in caps.rs is tagged '(M15)' but is a 'procedural skill' (procedural-memory concept), NOT the shared-memory object — do not overload it; add a distinct ObjKind::Block (append after Generic to keep existing discriminants stable since M_OBJECT_INSPECT returns kind as u64).

Adding the `block` field to caps::Object touches all 4 existing Object{} literals (mint, mint_memory_home, mint_record, mint_channel_endpoint) — each needs `block:None` or it won't compile; the dispatch arms must clone the Rc out and drop the slot borrow BEFORE touching the block (the M_MEM_*/M_CHAN_* single-&mut-at-a-time discipline) to avoid a reentrant borrow.
